info.title: dotnet-testx
info.description:
Extensions to the `dotnet test` command that enable code coverage reporting (using OpenCover) and a few other bonus features. 

NOTE: Due to OpenCover being Windows only, this tool will only work on Windows

Features

1. Run `dotnet test` with code coverage tracking by OpenCover
2. Generate cobertura covberage results (useful for VSTS/TFS coverage reporting). 
3. Generate an HTML coverage report
4. Discover all test projects in a folder and run tests for all of them (not natively supported by `dotnet test`). Works nicely with `dotnet watch` for continuous testing of an entire solution

See https://github.com/duanemck/dotnet-testx for more details

options.name: --discover-projects
options.description:
Discover all files in the working directory
matching the pattern and run tests on them. Alias
for '--project all'

options.name: --project
options.description:
Project to test. If you specify 'all' then it
will find all projects in the folder and
subfolders matching '*Tests.csproj'

options.name: --browser
options.description:
(Default: false) If generating an HTML report,
open in the default browser

options.name: --test-results-format
options.description:
(Default: trx) The format to use for the VSTest
test results

options.name: --opencover-filters
options.description:
(Default: +[*]*) Filters for opencover, i.e.
files to include/exclude from report

options.name: --opencover-mergeresults
options.description:
(Default: true) Merge multiple runs into the same
results file

options.name: --opencover-options
options.description:
(Default: ) Any other options you'd like passed
into OpenCover

options.name: --verbose
options.description:
(Default: false) Log more verbose details of
whats happening

options.name: --working-dir
options.description: Sets the working directory of the application
